title: luzula mannii subsp. gracilis
intro: Luzula mannii subsp. gracilis, also known as Luzula gracilis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sedge family (Juncaceae). It is native to New Zealand and is characterized by its slender stems, grass-like leaves, and yellowish-green flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Luzula gracilis can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and has been used to promote healing, reduce swelling, and support overall health and well-being.
